pr.title: Bump objdiff to v3.0.0 and wibo to 0.7.0
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated repository tooling around decomp comparison and Windows-exe execution: objdiff was bumped from v2.7.1 to v3.0.0, wibo from 0.6.16 to 0.7.0, nixpkgs was advanced, and supporting Nix packaging fixes were added. The PR mattered because objdiff 3 changed reported fuzzy/matched/linked percentages and how hard-coded labels are handled, initially making the diff bot appear to show broken matches. Review discussion concluded the apparent linked-code regression was expected/benign after updating the branch, with remaining reported issues being global jump table labels in GXPerf rather than real function breakage.
what_changed: Bumped objdiff in both Nix packaging and configure.py from v2.7.1 to v3.0.0.
Bumped wibo in both Nix packaging and configure.py from 0.6.16 to 0.7.0.
Updated .nix/sources.json to a newer nixpkgs revision and hash.
Changed .nix/objdiff.nix to fetch objdiff through srcOnly with a local patch, build the Rust workspace while excluding objdiff-wasm, and share those flags with tests.
Added .nix/duplicate-similar-dep.patch to patch Cargo.lock so duplicate similar 2.7.0 dependencies collapse to a single dependency entry.
Added cargoLock.outputHashes for git-sourced Rust dependencies gimli-0.32.0 and similar-2.7.0.
Updated .nix/wibo.nix for wibo 0.7.0, adding mimalloc as a build input and setting CMake flag -DFETCHCONTENT_TRY_FIND_PACKAGE_MODE=ALWAYS.
Adjusted .nix/devkitppc.nix to fetch the binutils patch from devkitPPC_r47 explicitly and changed wrapper generation to iterate only over bintools' bin directory rather than both gcc and bintools.
Patched .nix/m2c.nix to remove the graphviz dependency line from pyproject.toml during postPatch.

Review excerpt references a .nix/melee.nix nativeBuildInputs discussion about adding devkitppc, but that file is not listed in the final changed_files slice, so it may have been removed or superseded before merge.
smart_moves: Kept configure.py tool tags synchronized with the Nix package versions so local configuration and reproducible Nix builds point at the same objdiff and wibo releases.
Used a local Cargo.lock patch for objdiff instead of accepting duplicate similar dependencies, making the Rust package easier for Nix to vendor/hash deterministically.
Built objdiff with explicit workspace flags and excluded objdiff-wasm, avoiding unnecessary or incompatible wasm build work for the native objdiff package.
Added explicit cargoLock.outputHashes for non-registry/git Rust sources, which is required for reproducible Nix Rust builds.
For wibo 0.7.0, provided mimalloc and forced CMake FetchContent to prefer find_package mode, reducing vendoring/build surprises under Nix.
Investigated scary diff-bot metric changes before merge instead of assuming a tool bump was harmless; the discussion separated objdiff reporting/label behavior from actual decomp regressions.
decomp_lessons: Tool upgrades can change match reports without changing codegen; objdiff 3.0.0 changed fuzzy percentages and handling/reporting around hard-coded labels.

A drop in reported linked-code percentage should normally be suspicious, but in this PR the evidence points to objdiff behavior around hard-coded labels and stale branch state rather than real broken matches.
Before merging comparison-tool bumps, inspect whether reported broken matches are real functions or labels/jump-table artifacts.
Keep PR branches current when interpreting diff-bot output; stale branches can make real functions appear broken and confuse tool-upgrade validation.
Global jump table labels, specifically mentioned in GXPerf, can surface as apparent breakage under objdiff reporting changes.
Nix packaging for Rust decomp tools may require lockfile surgery and output hashes when upstream Cargo.lock mixes registry and git versions of the same crate.
naming_conventions: Tool version fields in configure.py use *_tag names such as objdiff_tag and wibo_tag.
Nix package definitions use pname/version with upstream release tags, e.g. pname = "objdiff" version = "3.0.0" and pname = "wibo" version = "0.7.0".
Local Nix patch files are named for the issue they address, e.g. duplicate-similar-dep.patch.
The devkitPPC release string is referenced explicitly as devkitPPC_r47 for the binutils patch URL.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Treat objdiff metric deltas after an objdiff upgrade as potentially algorithm/reporting-related, especially fuzzy percentages.
When linked-code percentage changes unexpectedly, verify with current branch state and inspect individual reported symbols before concluding there is a code match regression.
Differentiate real function match failures from hard-coded labels and global jump table labels in reports.
Use diff-bot output as a signal requiring triage, not as definitive proof of decomp breakage after comparison-tool upgrades.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that the diff algorithm seemed to change because fuzzy percentages differed, but questioned why many matches appeared broken.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged that linked-code percentage going down should normally be unaffected and suggested investigating the diff bot before merge.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Explained that the linked-code/report change was likely due to objdiff handling of hard-coded labels, recalling similar behavior from a prior beta.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: After updating the branch, concluded apparent real-function breakage was stale-branch noise and remaining reports were global jump table labels in GXPerf; considered the PR good to merge unless more testing was desired.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether adding devkitppc to nativeBuildInputs was still needed because they had been trying to factor it out.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Answered that devkitppc was not needed but was nice for development.

follow_up_queries: Find future PRs mentioning objdiff hard-coded labels or global jump table labels to see whether reporting behavior changed again.
Check whether .nix/melee.nix ultimately kept devkitppc in nativeBuildInputs or whether it was factored out after this review discussion.
Search for later objdiff bumps to compare required Cargo.lock patching and cargoLock.outputHashes patterns.
Look for GXPerf jump table label issues in diff-bot reports after objdiff v3.0.0.
